title: Inspired by the Story of Oreo, a Pomeranian Reunited with His Owner After Surviving for Five Days in the Debris of the Palisades Fire, the FOUND Act Not Only Protects Pets but Also
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: The FOUND Act ensures that local governments have clear rescue plans in place for pets during emergencies.
The FOUND Act requires cities and counties to update emergency plans to designate procedures for the rescue of pets in mandatory evacuation zones.
The FOUND Act directs local governments to provide online resources on pet evacuation and reunification assistance.
The FOUND Act guarantees that any pet rescued from an evacuated area is held for at least 90 days before being adopted out, euthanized, or transferred.
The 90-day holding period for rescued pets is an improvement from the current 72-hour minimum.
The FOUND Act aims to prevent pet owners from risking their lives to save their animals during evacuations.
Increasing wildfire threats in California necessitate disaster response plans that include the safety and well-being of animals.
